---
title: Sampling
description: Samples, populations, and variability.
---

::: {.learning-objectives}
### Learning Objectives

- Distinguish sample and population.
- Explain sampling variability.
- Recognize bias.
:::

A sample is a subset of a population. Good samples help us learn about the
population without measuring everyone.

::: {.practice-box}
1. What is a population?
2. What is sampling bias?
3. Why do random samples help?
:::

## Solutions

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 1

Answer: the full group or process we want to learn about.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 2

Answer: a systematic way the sample differs from the population.

Bias can make estimates wrong even when the sample is large.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 3

Answer: random sampling reduces systematic selection patterns and supports
probability-based uncertainty estimates.
:::
